title: Elements in a container should be erased with std::erase or std::erase_if
description: Removing elements with a specific value or that follow a given predicate from a container is a common task. Before C++20, this was not straightforward. The way to do it had to depend on the type of your container.
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

checks.message: Use std::erase or std::erase_if instead of erase-remove idiom.

remove_value.description: Remove elements by value using erase-remove idiom

remove_if_predicate.description: Remove elements by predicate using erase-remove_if idiom
